ddls:
CREATE TABLE "survey_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set of scores */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "f1_score" REAL /* Score on the first factor (f1) */,
  "f2_score" REAL /* Score on the second factor (f2) */,
  "f3_score" REAL /* Score on the third factor (f3) */,
  "f4_score" REAL /* Score on the fourth factor (f4) */,
  "f5_score" REAL /* Score on the fifth factor (f5) */,
  "f6_score" REAL /* Score on the sixth factor (f6) */,
  "f7_score" REAL /* Score on the seventh factor (f7) */,
  "scr_score" INTEGER /* Score on the SCR test (a cognitive assessment) */,
  "fac_score" INTEGER /* Score on the FAC test (a cognitive assessment) */,
  "lon_score" INTEGER /* Score on the LON test (a cognitive assessment) */,
  "eff_score" INTEGER /* Score on the EFF test (a cognitive assessment) */,
  "d_score" REAL /* Score on the D test (a cognitive assessment) */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the scores were obtained */,
  "test_location" TEXT /* Location where the test was conducted */,
  "test_conductor" TEXT /* Name or ID of the person who conducted the test */,
  "additional_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cognitive_tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cognitive test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the cognitive test */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cognitive test */,
  "min_score" INTEGER /* Minimum possible score for the test */,
  "max_score" INTEGER /* Maximum possible score for the test */,
  "test_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the test in minutes */,
  "test_difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level of the test */,
  "test_version" TEXT /* Version of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic category */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex (1: Male, 2: Female) */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level (1: No formal education, 2: Primary, 3: Secondary, 4: Higher education) */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level (1: Very low, 2: Low, 3: Medium, 4: High, 5: Very high, 6: Extremely high) */,
  "parental_marital_status" TEXT /* Parental marital status (1: Married, 2: Divorced, 3: Single) */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the participant */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or state where the participant is from */,
  "urban_rural" TEXT /* Whether the participant lives in an urban or rural area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each set of scores
